top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: temporal-progression-lab
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Temporal metadata and trend dynamics appear as a critical gap across general-purpose analysis, while pickle-idea-lab has already identified 73 players with 5+ longitudinal clips. This deserves dedicated infrastructure to model skill progression, seasonal patterns, and viral trajectory dynamics as independent phenomena.

Evidence: general-purpose gap_finder identifies temporal metadata gap; pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout flags longitudinal tracking feasibility (73 players, 5+ clips); wildcard findings on skill consistency (r=0.90) suggest progression patterns worth tracking over time
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py temporal-progression-lab — then customize config. Suggested researchers: time-series analyst, sports science methodologist, retention data scientist

proposal.rationale: Temporal metadata and trend dynamics appear as a critical gap across general-purpose analysis, while pickle-idea-lab has already identified 73 players with 5+ longitudinal clips. This deserves dedicated infrastructure to model skill progression, seasonal patterns, and viral trajectory dynamics as independent phenomena.
proposal.evidence: general-purpose gap_finder identifies temporal metadata gap; pickle-idea-lab opportunity_scout flags longitudinal tracking feasibility (73 players, 5+ clips); wildcard findings on skill consistency (r=0.90) suggest progression patterns worth tracking over time
proposal.business_context: Longitudinal skill tracking unlocks retention prediction, personalized coaching cadence, and sponsorship tier eligibility signals—turning one-off clips into career narratives that justify premium memberships.

draft_config.description: Temporal metadata and trend dynamics appear as a critical gap across general-purpose analysis, while pickle-idea-lab has already identified 73 players with 5+ longitudinal clips. This deserves dedicated infrastructure to model skill progression, seasonal patterns, and viral trajectory dynamics as independent phenomena.
draft_config.business_context: Longitudinal skill tracking unlocks retention prediction, personalized coaching cadence, and sponsorship tier eligibility signals—turning one-off clips into career narratives that justify premium memberships.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Requires lab infrastructure setup, regulatory approval for experimental protocols, and specialized equipment—far exceeds 30-day solo feasibility.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Evidence would strengthen with: (a) quantitative gap analysis showing temporal metadata coverage % across existing labs, (b) concrete examples of missed insights due to temporal analysis absence, (c) comparison of temporal-progression-lab's projected output against pickle-idea-lab's 73 players to show non-redundancy. Currently relies on assertion of 'critical gap' without baseline metrics.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: The 73 players identified by pickle-idea-lab could already contain temporal-progression patterns. No evidence shown that existing labs *cannot* surface temporal insights. Risk of lab proliferation without demonstrating that proposed lab adds orthogonal value rather than duplicating analysis across different framing.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: temporal-progression-lab'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py temporal-progression-lab — then customize config. Suggested researchers: time-series analyst, sports science methodologist, retention data scientist

top_strategic.title: No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs
top_strategic.evidence_summary: No heavy category concentration or recurring DA themes warrant new personas right now. Will re-check as more runs accumulate.
top_strategic.recommended_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.
top_strategic.plausibility_note: This is an analytical finding, not a buildable product—no prototype possible without first identifying and validating actual persona gaps.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is appropriately conservative. Change mind if: (a) subsequent runs show DA theme clustering in specific labs, (b) new personas emerge from accumulated data showing systematic coverage gaps, (c) user feedback indicates unmet analytical personas. The 'will re-check' commitment is good practice.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Assumption that 2 labs provide sufficient sample size to detect persona gaps. Unknown: whether the lab selection itself biases toward similar persona needs. If labs are too similar in scope, true gaps may be invisible. Also doesn't surface whether *absence* of persona concentration is itself meaningful (could indicate either: good coverage OR insufficient specialization).

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.

top_data.title: Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs
top_data.evidence_summary: The finding category 'wildcard_idea' is being produced by multiple labs: {'general-purpose': 8, 'pickle-idea-lab': 8}. This suggests a cross-cutting pattern type.
top_data.recommended_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Evidence needs: (a) semantic analysis—are these 16 instances the *same* wildcard_idea or different ideas both tagged 'wildcard'?, (b) temporal distribution—are they clustering or evenly dispersed?, (c) downstream impact—are duplicate wildcard findings causing decision confusion or are they complementary signals? Current evidence is purely distributional.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Doesn't distinguish between healthy *convergent validation* (two independent labs surfacing the same insight = stronger signal) vs. problematic *duplication* (redundant work). Also doesn't explain *why* wildcard_idea is appearing—is this a config artifact, genuine cross-cutting theme, or catch-all category for unclassified findings? The incompleteness of the evidence sentence ('This suggests a cross-cutting p...') suggests analysis itself is incomplete.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

top_data.title: No lab subscriptions configured
top_data.evidence_summary: No labs have declared subscriptions yet. Add `subscriptions: ["lab-id:researcher"]` to a lab's config to enable cross-lab signal routing.
top_data.recommended_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is a factual observation, not a claim requiring validation. Would change mind only if: (a) evidence of subscriptions existing but not being read, (b) configuration being ignored despite being present. The recommendation is sound.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Doesn't probe *why* subscriptions aren't configured—is this: (a) unknown feature, (b) unclear value proposition, (c) absence of routing targets, (d) intentional isolation? Without understanding the cause, the recommendation may not be adopted. Also doesn't show what *would* happen if subscriptions were configured—what new routing would emerge, and is that desirable?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No lab subscriptions configured'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.
